questions.question: Find the area of a triangle whose two sides are 16 cm and 24 cm and these two sides make an angle of 60⁰ with each other?
questions.explanation: for any triangle, when 2 sides and angle between them is given, the area of that triangle = (½)a*b*sin C Area = (½)*16*24*sin60⁰ = 96√3 sq. cm

questions.question: The diagonals of two squares are in the ratio 3:7. Find the ratio of their areas.
questions.explanation: Let the diagonals of the square be 3x and 7x respectively. Area of square = (diagonal) ² /2 Ratio of their areas = [½(3x)²]:[½(7x)²] = 9 : 49

questions.question: If a side of the square is increased by 4 cm to make it a rectangle, its area increased by 36 cm². Find the area of the square?
questions.explanation: Let the side of the square = x cm Area = x² Now, length is increased by 4 cm, L = (x + 4) and B = x cm Area = (x + 4) x Now, (x + 4)x - x² = 36 4x = 36 x = 9 Area = 9² = 81cm²

questions.question: If (x-5) (y-7) (z-3) = 1000, then the least possible value of (x + y + z) equals?
After selling an article at 30% discount, a man gets 20% profit. Find the profit percentage if the discount is 9%.

If numbers of males and females in town P are equal and number of females in town Q is 50% more than the number of males in town Q, then what is the difference between the average age of males and females together in town P and the average age of males and females together in town Q?
questions.explanation: Let the number of males in town P = x Number of females in town P = Number of males in town P = x Let the number of males in town Q = y Number of females in town Q = 1.5y Average age of males and females together in town P = (50x + 40x)/2x = 45 years. Average age of males and females together in town Q = (45y + (35 * 1.5y))/ (2.5y) = (45y + 52.5y)/2.5y = 39 years. Required difference = 45 -39 = 6 years.

questions.question: If the number of females in each of the given town is equal, then what is the average age of all females in given 6 towns?
questions.explanation: Let number of females in each town be N Average age = ((40 +35 +25 +28 +27 +22) x N)/(6N) = 177/6 = 29.5 years

questions.question: If ratio of the number of males to females in town R is 2:3, then what is the average age of males and females together in town R?
questions.explanation: Let the numbers of males and females in town R be 2N and 3N respectively. Required average age = ((30 x 2N) + (25 x 3N))/(5N) =(135N/5N) = 27 years.

questions.question: If the average age of males and females together in town S is 26.2 years and the number of males in town S is 10 more than the number of females, then what is the total number of males and females together in town S?
questions.explanation: Let the number of females in town S be N Number of males in town S = (N +10) Total age of males and females together in town S = (N+10) * 25 + (N * 28) = 25N +250 +28N =53N +250 Also, total age of males and females together in town S= (2N +10) x 26.2 = 52.4N +262 53N +250 =52.4N +262 0.6N =12 N =20 Total number of males and females in town S = 20 +30 = 50.

questions.question: What will be the remainder when (6x² - 11x + 8) is divided by (3x + 2)?
Ratio of boys and girls in a school is 5:6. If boys are increased by 10% and girls are increased by 20%, Find the respective ratio of boys and girls.
Ravina got 35% marks in an examination out of 500 marks and failed by 15 marks. Find the percentage of pass marks.
questions.explanation: Pass marks = [(35/100) x 500] + 15 = 175 + 15 = 190 Percentage of pass marks = (190/500) x 100 = 38%

questions.question: If x = 3 + 2√2, what is the value of x³ + 1/x³?
(sec² x-cosec²x) [ cos²x (sinx+cosx)_________cosec²x (sinx-cosx) + sin²x (sinx-cosx)_________sec² x(sinx+cosx) ] =
questions.explanation: (sec² x - cosec² x) = (sin² x - cos² x)/(sin² x cos² x) (cos² x(sinx+cosx)/(cosec² x(sinx-cosx))+(sin² x(sinx-cosx))/(sec² x(sinx+cosx))(sec² x-cosec² x) = (sinx + cosx)² + (sinx - cosx)² = 2(sin²x + cos²x) = 2

questions.question: If tan A = 1 - cos B_________sin B , then tan 2A is equal to:
questions.explanation: tan A = 1 - cos B_________sin B = 2 sin² (B/2)_________2 sin (B/2) cos (B/2) = sin (B/2)_________cos (B/2) = tan (B/2) So, A = B/2 and 2A = B so tan (2A) = tan B

questions.question: Find the value of (Sin³A - Cos³A)/Sin A - Cos A = ?
questions.explanation: (Sin³A - Cos³A) = (Sin A - Cos A) (Sin²A + Cos A Sin A +Cos²A) So, (Sin³A - Cos³A)/sin A - Cos A = (Sin A - Cos A) (Sin²A + Cos A Sin A +Cos²A) / (Sin A - Cos A) = 1 + cos A sin A

questions.question: By what least number 675 be multiplied to obtain a number which is a perfect cube?
questions.explanation: 675 = 5x5x3x3x3 It is clear that if 675 is multiplied by 5, then resultant will be perfect cube.

questions.question: In a circle, distance between two parallel chords is 30 cm. The lengths of the chords are 30 cm and 26 cm. What is the radius of the circle?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p795/image001.png) Let the chords be AB and CD. Also, let r be the radius of the circle. Let OE = x, then OF = 30 - x A perpendicular drawn from the centre to a chord bisects it. Thus, AE = 26/2 = 13 cm and DF = 30/2 = 15 cm In triangle AOE, AO² = AE² + OE² r² = 13² + x² ....(1) In triangle DOF, OD² = OF² + DF² r² = (30 - x)² + 15² ....(2) Solving equations (1) and (2), 13² + x² = (30 - x)² + 15² x = 16 cm Using equation (1), r² = 13² + 16² = 20.6 cm Hence, Option 2 is correct.

questions.question: A person invested his amount in two schemes. In first he gains a profit of 60% whereas in second he has a loss of 25%. Total profit in the first business is Rs 1878. What is the total investment of that person if he gains 28% profit over all?(approximate to nearest integer)
questions.explanation: Let the first amount is x and the second is 100 - x (1.6x) + {0.75*(100-x)} = 1.28*100 Or x = 62.35 Profit = 62.35 * 60/100 = 37.41 in every 100 Rs Total investment = 100 * (1878/37.41) = Rs 5020

questions.question: Find the compound interest on Rs 7000 at 20% per annum for 2 years if the interest is compounded semi-annually.
A building 47 m long and 34 m broad is surrounded by a garden of uniform width of 1.5 m. find the cost of levelling the garden at the rate of Rs. 2.8 per sq. metre.
questions.explanation: External dimensions = 47 + 2x1.5 and 34 + 2x1.5 = 50 and 37 Area of garden = external area - area of building = 50 x 37 - 47 x 34 = 1850 - 1598 = 252 sq m Cost of levelling = 252 x 2.8 = Rs 705.6

questions.question: If Ratish, Madhav and Vinay can complete a project in 20, 30 and 60 days respectively. In how many days they can complete the whole project if Madhav and Vinay helps him on every fourth day ?
questions.explanation: Ratish can do 1/20 = 5% of the work per day Madhav can do 1/30 = 3.33% of the work per day Vinay can do 1/60 = 1.66% of the work per day In four days Ratish can do 20 % of the job himself and together Madhav and Vinay does 5% of the project (1.66% + 3.33%) => In four days all of them together can do 25% of the project Therefore, to complete 100% project, they need [ 4 X (100 /25) ] = 16 days. Hence, option 2.

questions.question: A train covered a distance of 1235 km in 19 hours. Also, the average speed of a car is four-fifths the average speed of the train. How much distance will the car cover in 22 hours?
questions.explanation: Average speed of train = 1235/19 = 65 km/h Average speed of car = ⅘ x 65 = 52 km/h Distance covered by the car in 22 hours = 52x22 = 1144 km

questions.question: The average of 8 numbers is 15. The average of first two numbers is 12 and that of the next three is 16. If the sixth number is less than the seventh and eighth numbers by 6 and 9 respectively, then the eighth number is
questions.explanation: Let the eighth number be x. Then, sixth number = x - 9 Also, sixth number = seventh number - 6 =>x - 9 = seventh number - 6 =>Seventh number = (x - 9) + 6 = (x - 3) So, (2 X 12)+(3 X 16)+ (x-9)+ (x-3)+ x=8 X 15 =>24 + 48 + 3x - 12 = 120 =>3x + 60 = 120 => x = 20

questions.question: Solve the expression: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/vpimages/DivisibilityHCFLCM/image011.png)
questions.explanation: Let 343 =a And 257 =b ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/vpimages/DivisibilityHCFLCM/image012.png) =343+257=600. Hence, the correct answer is option (2).
